mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower corporate profits and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising retail sales and declining interest rates amplifies consumer purchasing power and business revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS increases while FEDFUNDS decreases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain efficiency, which boosts near-term corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during recovery phases, or the predictive IC drops below 0.01 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, which systematically translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to correlate with 60-day equity returns, or the signal produces negative alpha after transaction costs in backtesting.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 210.0, it indicates entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 210.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts confirms broad-based economic expansion and rising household wealth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that immediately triggers risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Consecutive INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in >50% of cases, or the signal shows no predictive power in high-frequency walk-forward tests.
